prompt:
I expect to run about 10,000 automated browser research sessions per month. Compare the current pricing, practical limits, and major differences between
  Browserbase, Browserless, and Steel, then recommend the best starting option. Clearly identify anything you could not verify and include sources.
finalResponse:
**Bottom Line**

For ~10,000 automated browser research sessions/month, I’d start with **Steel Launch** if your sessions are usually short and you can live with **10 concurrent browsers / 15-minute max sessions**. It has the lowest public metered cost: **$0/month + $0.10/browser-hour**, with **$30 one-time credits**, so 10,000 one-minute sessions would be about **167 browser-hours = ~$16.70** before proxy/captcha/tool extras. Source: [Steel Pricing/Limits](https://docs.steel.dev/overview/pricinglimits).

If you want the safest general-purpose starting point with fewer session-duration surprises, start with **Browserbase Developer**: **$20/month**, **100 included browser-hours**, **25 concurrency**, **6-hour sessions**, then **$0.12/browser-hour overage**. Same 10,000 one-minute workload lands around **$48/month** before proxy. Source: [Browserbase Plans](https://docs.browserbase.com/account/billing/plans.md).

| Provider | Best Public Starter For 10k Sessions | Public Pricing Model | Key Limits | Main Difference |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| **Steel** | **Launch** | $0 + usage; browser hours **$0.10/hr**, proxy **$10/GB**, captcha **$3/1k**, browser tools **$5/1k** | Launch: **10 concurrent**, **15 min max session**, **60 rpm**, 7-day retention | Cheapest short-session option; open-source/agent-focused; Scale is $250 + usage |
| **Browserbase** | **Developer** or Startup | Developer **$20/mo**, 100 hrs, overage **$0.12/hr**; Startup **$99/mo**, 500 hrs, overage **$0.10/hr** | Developer: **25 concurrent**, **6 hr sessions**, **25 creates/min**; Startup: **100 concurrent**, **50 creates/min** | Most balanced managed browser platform; good caps for research agents |
| **Browserless** | **Prototyping** if short sessions | Prototyping **$25/mo billed annually**, **20k units**, overage **$0.002/unit**; Starter **$140/mo billed annually**, 180k units | Prototyping: **10 concurrent**, **15 min max**; 1 unit per **30 sec** browser time | Strongest public stealth/BQL story; pricing is unit-based, proxies/captchas can climb fast |

**How 10,000 Sessions Price Out**

Assuming **1 minute/session**, no built-in proxy, no captcha solving:

| Provider | Rough Monthly Cost | Notes |
|---|---:|---|
| **Steel Launch** | **~$16.70** after free credits are gone | 166.7 hrs × $0.10/hr; Launch cap is the catch |
| **Browserbase Developer** | **~$48** | $20 + 66.7 overage hrs × $0.12/hr |
| **Browserbase Startup** | **$99** | Includes 500 hrs, better concurrency |
| **Browserless Prototyping** | **$25 billed annually** | 10,000 min = 20,000 units, exactly the included amount |
| **Browserless Starter** | **$140 billed annually** | More comfortable if sessions exceed 1 min or concurrency matters |

**Recommendation**

Start with **Steel Launch** for a pilot if your research jobs are mostly short, controlled, and you do not need long-lived sessions. It gives you the cheapest real test of 10k/month volume.

Move to **Browserbase Developer/Startup** if your research sessions sometimes run long, you need more predictable browser session ergonomics, or you want better headroom without immediately jumping to a $250 plan.

Pick **Browserless** first only if stealth, CAPTCHA handling, BrowserQL, or multi-browser support like Chrome/WebKit/Firefox is central to the workload. Its unit model is clean for short runs, but less forgiving when sessions idle, proxy traffic grows, or pages are slow.

**Could Not Verify**

I could not verify exact **monthly non-annual Browserless prices** from the extracted public page; the visible pricing I captured was “billed annually.” I also did not independently benchmark uptime, success rates, anti-bot effectiveness, or support quality; the comparison above is based on current public pricing/docs only.

Whether you're collecting data at scale, running parallel tests, or serving multiple users, you'll need to understand concurrency and rate limits. Browser time is billed in **30-second increments**. Every 30 seconds a browser session is open consumes **1 unit**. Partial increments are rounded up, so a session open for 31 seconds consumes **2 units**. This applies to all connection methods: BrowserQL, BaaS (Puppeteer/Playwright), and REST APIs. A session that runs for 45 seconds consumes 2 units, regardless of whether the browser is actively navigating or sitting idle. Close sessions as soon as you're done to avoid unnecessary consumption. > **Set a custom `timeout` on your connections to prevent runaway sessions from draining your balance. details.text:
**Bottom Line**

For ~10,000 automated browser research sessions/month, I’d start with **Steel Launch** if your sessions are usually short and you can live with **10 concurrent browsers / 15-minute max sessions**. It has the lowest public metered cost: **$0/month + $0.10/browser-hour**, with **$30 one-time credits**, so 10,000 one-minute sessions would be about **167 browser-hours = ~$16.70** before proxy/captcha/tool extras. Source: [Steel Pricing/Limits](https://docs.steel.dev/overview/pricinglimits).

If you want the safest general-purpose starting point with fewer session-duration surprises, start with **Browserbase Developer**: **$20/month**, **100 included browser-hours**, **25 concurrency**, **6-hour sessions**, then **$0.12/browser-hour overage**. Same 10,000 one-minute workload lands around **$48/month** before proxy. Source: [Browserbase Plans](https://docs.browserbase.com/account/billing/plans.md).

| Provider | Best Public Starter For 10k Sessions | Public Pricing Model | Key Limits | Main Difference |
|---|---:|---|---|---|
| **Steel** | **Launch** | $0 + usage; browser hours **$0.10/hr**, proxy **$10/GB**, captcha **$3/1k**, browser tools **$5/1k** | Launch: **10 concurrent**, **15 min max session**, **60 rpm**, 7-day retention | Cheapest short-session option; open-source/agent-focused; Scale is $250 + usage |
| **Browserbase** | **Developer** or Startup | Developer **$20/mo**, 100 hrs, overage **$0.12/hr**; Startup **$99/mo**, 500 hrs, overage **$0.10/hr** | Developer: **25 concurrent**, **6 hr sessions**, **25 creates/min**; Startup: **100 concurrent**, **50 creates/min** | Most balanced managed browser platform; good caps for research agents |
| **Browserless** | **Prototyping** if short sessions | Prototyping **$25/mo billed annually**, **20k units**, overage **$0.002/unit**; Starter **$140/mo billed annually**, 180k units | Prototyping: **10 concurrent**, **15 min max**; 1 unit per **30 sec** browser time | Strongest public stealth/BQL story; pricing is unit-based, proxies/captchas can climb fast |

**How 10,000 Sessions Price Out**

Assuming **1 minute/session**, no built-in proxy, no captcha solving:

| Provider | Rough Monthly Cost | Notes |
|---|---:|---|
| **Steel Launch** | **~$16.70** after free credits are gone | 166.7 hrs × $0.10/hr; Launch cap is the catch |
| **Browserbase Developer** | **~$48** | $20 + 66.7 overage hrs × $0.12/hr |
| **Browserbase Startup** | **$99** | Includes 500 hrs, better concurrency |
| **Browserless Prototyping** | **$25 billed annually** | 10,000 min = 20,000 units, exactly the included amount |
| **Browserless Starter** | **$140 billed annually** | More comfortable if sessions exceed 1 min or concurrency matters |

**Recommendation**

Start with **Steel Launch** for a pilot if your research jobs are mostly short, controlled, and you do not need long-lived sessions. It gives you the cheapest real test of 10k/month volume.

Move to **Browserbase Developer/Startup** if your research sessions sometimes run long, you need more predictable browser session ergonomics, or you want better headroom without immediately jumping to a $250 plan.

Pick **Browserless** first only if stealth, CAPTCHA handling, BrowserQL, or multi-browser support like Chrome/WebKit/Firefox is central to the workload. Its unit model is clean for short runs, but less forgiving when sessions idle, proxy traffic grows, or pages are slow.

**Could Not Verify**

I could not verify exact **monthly non-annual Browserless prices** from the extracted public page; the visible pricing I captured was “billed annually.” I also did not independently benchmark uptime, success rates, anti-bot effectiveness, or support quality; the comparison above is based on current public pricing/docs only.
